titleStmt.title: Letter from Andrew Philip Magill to Sir Bertram Coghill Alan Windle, 1 April 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Andrew Philip Magill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter from Andrew Philip Magill (1871-1941) was sent to Sir Bertram Coghill Alan Windle (1858–1929). Magill was a civil servant and held the position of private secretary for successive chief secretaries of Ireland until his transfer to the Northern Irish Civil Service in 1921. Windle was a scholar and university president in Cork. He came up against Sinn Féin resistance on numerous occasions as he was accused of trying to consolidate pro-British, pro-unionist control of University College Cork. Alfred O'Rahilly (1884–1969) was one of the men involved in mobilizing anti-Windle opinion for personal and political reasons. The letter was an acknowledgement of Windle's recommendation on the behalf of Sir John Robert O'Connell (1868-1943) to the position of chief crown solicitor.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7153/12

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Copy 1st April, 1916. Dear Sir Bertram, I am desired by Mr. Birrell to acknowledge the
receipt of your letter of 30th ultimo recommending
Sir J. R. O'Connell for appointment as Chief Crown
Solicitor for Ireland, and to say that Mr. Birrell will
see that his application is fully considered. Yours sincerely, (Sd.) APM
Under Secy.
A.P.M 1/4/16.
Sir Bertrum C. A. Windle, LL.D., Listarkin,
Union Hall,
Co.Cork.
